params.prompt: Photorealistic interior photograph of a medium Relax, outdoor/covered patio area with wide openings to living/dining windows, Patio is outdoor/covered — use natural daylight as primary light; avoid adding interior-style lamps outdoors. Show soft directional daylight coming through patio openings into living/dining when applicable., japandi aesthetic, warm_neutral color palette, balanced furnishing, A calm Japandi interior that balances Scandinavian minimalism and Japanese restraint with warm, earthy materials. Emphasis on low, horizontal furniture lines, layered natural textures (wood, linen, woven rattan), and a restrained palette of warm neutrals to create a cozy, meditative home for a young family. Integrate a small work corner in the living area and a crib in the bedroom while keeping clutter minimal., Covered patio with simple seating: low wooden bench, floor-level cushions or small tea table, a couple of potted sculptural plants. Maintain continuity with interior materials (same oak tone). Camera framing: slightly wider 24–35mm to capture opening relationship to living/dining. Ensure outdoor scale reads correctly and that patio floor and ceiling lines are parallel to frame., must include floor-level cushions, low wooden bench, must avoid High-gloss finishes, bright saturated colors, and heavy patterning, Overstuffed furniture or high-back sofas that break the low-line language, Artificial-looking greenery and oversized plants that scale incorrectly, Visible modern tech clutter (do not stage visible charging cables, multiple monitors), introducing interior lamps outdoors, oversized tropical plants, people, visible brand logos, architectural photography style, Canon 5D, 35mm lens, professional staging, high resolution, sharp focus, photo taken for a luxury property listing Create one photorealistic staged Relax still for a premium real-estate listing. Maintain believable residential scale for Relax at roughly medium. Use japandi styling, warm_neutral palette, soft_ambient lighting, and balanced furnishing density. A calm Japandi interior that balances Scandinavian minimalism and Japanese restraint with warm, earthy materials. Emphasis on low, horizontal furniture lines, layered natural textures (wood, linen, woven rattan), and a restrained palette of warm neutrals to create a cozy, meditative home for a young family. Integrate a small work corner in the living area and a crib in the bedroom while keeping clutter minimal. Patio is outdoor/covered — use natural daylight as primary light; avoid adding interior-style lamps outdoors. Show soft directional daylight coming through patio openings into living/dining when applicable. Must include: floor-level cushions, low wooden bench. Must avoid: High-gloss finishes, bright saturated colors, and heavy patterning, Overstuffed furniture or high-back sofas that break the low-line language, Artificial-looking greenery and oversized plants that scale incorrectly, Visible modern tech clutter (do not stage visible charging cables, multiple monitors), introducing interior lamps outdoors, oversized tropical plants, people, visible brand logos. Keep these fixed unless the user explicitly wants transformation: exact architecture room geometry furniture layout lens realism vertical line integrity believable scale light entering from visible sources only
